title: MCP Embed Tools for CRUD operations on embed resources
purpose: Provides functions to create, list, and delete embed resources such as iframes and Storybook previews, with support for Storybook-specific configurations.
module_context: Defines API constants for host and port, URL building utility, and interfaces for result types. Exports schemas for validation of input parameters for various embed operations.

methods.name: buildUrl
methods.normalized_params: (path: string, project: string, session: string, extraParams?: Record<string, string>): string

steps.content: Construct a base URL using the provided path and API base URL.
Set the project parameter in the URL search parameters.
Set the session parameter in the URL search parameters.
If extra parameters are provided, add each key-value pair to the URL search parameters.
Return the fully constructed URL string.

methods.name: handleCreateEmbed
methods.normalized_params: (project: string, session: string, name: string, url: string, subtype?: string, width?: string, height?: string, storybook?: { storyId: string; port: number }): Promise<CreateEmbedResult>

steps.content: Prepare a request body with the provided name and URL.
Add subtype, width, height, and storybook properties to the request body if they are defined.
Send a POST request to the embed API endpoint with the constructed body.
IF the response is not successful, parse the error and throw a new error with the message.
IF the response is successful, parse the JSON response and return an object with success status and the ID.
Send a GET request to the embeds API endpoint.
IF the response is not successful, throw an error with the status text.
IF the response is successful, parse the JSON response and return an object with the embeds array.

methods.name: handleDeleteEmbed
methods.normalized_params: (project: string, session: string, id: string): Promise<DeleteEmbedResult>

steps.content: Send a DELETE request to the specific embed API endpoint using the provided ID.
IF the response is not successful, parse the error and throw a new error with the message.
IF the response is successful, return an object with a success status.

methods.name: handleCreateStorybookEmbed
methods.normalized_params: (project: string, session: string, name: string, storyId: string, port?: number, host?: string): Promise<CreateEmbedResult>

steps.content: Determine the actual port and host for the Storybook server, defaulting to 6006 and localhost if not provided.
Construct the URL for the Storybook iframe using the actual host and port.
Delegate to the create embed handler with the constructed URL and storybook-specific configuration.

methods.name: handleListStorybookStories
methods.normalized_params: (port?: number, host?: string): Promise<{ stories?: Array<{ id: string; title: string; name: string; importPath: string }>; error?: string }>

steps.content: Determine the actual port and host for the Storybook server, defaulting to 6006 and localhost if not provided.
Construct the index URL for the Storybook server.
Send a GET request to the Storybook index URL.
IF the response is not successful, return an error message indicating the server could not be reached.
IF the response is successful, parse the JSON and filter entries to include only those of type 'story'.
Map the filtered entries to extract story ID, title, name, and import path.
Return an object containing the extracted stories.
